title: [PAPER] A Variational Qubit-Efficient MaxCut Heuristic Algorithm
why:
- The Qubit-Efficient MaxCut (QEMC) algorithm enables the MaxCut problem to be solved with only \(O(\log N)\) qubits for a graph of size N, representing an exponential reduction in qubit requirements compared to QAOA.  
- The QEMC algorithm demonstrated performance on 32-node graph instances using just 5 qubits on real superconducting quantum hardware, and simulations on graphs with up to 2048 nodes using 11 qubits.  
- The algorithm's innovative encoding scheme provides strong noise resilience and allows for efficient classical simulation.  
- QEMC establishes a challenging benchmark for QAOA on noisy quantum devices and introduces a novel quantum-inspired approach to combinatorial optimization.
